disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No honey-coloured purulent crusts, no spreading warm induration, no fever, no lymphadenopathy (against impetigo/cellulitis)
trigger_question_examples: Any thick yellow (honey-coloured) weeping crusts or pus, spreading hot redness, or fever?
Any tender lumps in your neck?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No honey-coloured crusts or pus, no spreading hot redness, no fever, and no neck lumps.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral silvery-scaled sharply-demarcated plaques (not a unilateral dermatomal vesicular band) worsened by a new harsh shampoo
trigger_question_examples: Are the patches on both sides with silvery scale, or a one-sided stripe of blisters?
Did it worsen after the new shampoo?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both sides with silvery scale, not a one-sided blister stripe, and it got worse after the new shampoo.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Is this infected? Will my hair fall out?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Explain the silvery-scaled, sharply-demarcated, afebrile, no-pus picture is a a scaly scalp condition flare aggravated by harsh shampoos, not an infection; advise stopping harsh shampoos, a topical steroid scalp lotion and gentle care, with a secondary-infection safety-net — without antibiotics/ED.
